title: Build MAP Retrospective/Learning System (end-of-cycle review loop)
description: Per Guidelines/MAP_repo_systems_gap_review.md secondary gap (weakest/implied): the improvement backlog and emergence capture exist but no formal end-of-cycle retrospective loop exists asking what worked, what failed, what caused rework, what agents misunderstood, what rules were unclear, what should become a validator/template/decision. Build MAP_System/RETROSPECTIVE_SYSTEM.md and MAP_System/templates/RETROSPECTIVE_TEMPLATE.md. This is the last system in the gap review's list -- it should also reflect on the just-completed gap-review build sequence itself (TASK-103 through TASK-117) as its first worked example: repeated review findings (output_paths omissions occurred 3 times before the pattern was internalized) are exactly the kind of repeated-failure-becomes-permanent-fix pattern this system should capture. Must cross-link Self-Repair (follow-up prevention is retrospective at incident scale, this system is retrospective at cycle scale), Emergence (recurring insights feed retrospectives), improvement-backlog.md (where retrospective findings that need follow-up land), and Change Control (a retrospective finding that becomes a validator is itself a change). Depends on TASK-117 (shared decisions.md touch point); do not submit until TASK-117 is released. Register all output_paths upfront including cross-link edits.
acceptance_criteria: MAP_System/RETROSPECTIVE_SYSTEM.md exists and defines the end-of-cycle retrospective loop: what worked, what failed, what caused rework, what agents misunderstood, what rules were unclear, what should become a validator/template/decision
RETROSPECTIVE_SYSTEM.md includes a worked retrospective of the TASK-103 through TASK-117 gap-review build sequence itself, naming the repeated output_paths-omission pattern as an example finding
RETROSPECTIVE_SYSTEM.md cross-links SELF_REPAIR_SYSTEM.md, emergence/README.md, shared/improvement-backlog.md, and CHANGE_CONTROL_SYSTEM.md
shared/decisions.md gets a new DECISION entry recording Retrospective System adoption
events/events.jsonl has PROGRESS/SUBMISSION entries tracing the build
